syntax errors\n//   - Handles empty files gracefully (returns empty map, not error)\n//   - Provides helpful error messages with line numbers for syntax errors\n//   - Uses the file I/O foundation reporting.\n125\tfunc (v *Validator) ValidateStringWithPath(yamlContent, filePath string) ValidationResult {\n126\t\tresult := ValidationResult{\n127\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n128\t\t\tValid:    true,\n129\t\t\tErrors:   []ValidationError{},\n130\t\t\tWarnings: []ValidationError{},\n131\t\t}\n132\t\n133\t\t// Check for empty content\n134\t\tif strings.TrimSpace(yamlContent) == \"\" ValidationError{\n189\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeSyntax,\n190\t\t\tMessage:  err.Error(),\n191\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n192\t\t}\n193\t\n194\t\t// Parse line and column from error message if available\n195\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n196\t\n197\t\t// Try to extract line number from common error patterns\n198\t\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"line \") {\n199\t\t\tparts := strings.Split(errMsg, \"line \")\n200\t\t\tif len(parts) > 1 {\n201\t\t\t\tlineStr := strings.Fields(parts[1])[0]\n202\t\t\t\tvar line int\n203\t\t\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(lineStr, \"%d\", &line); err == nil {\n204\t\t\t\t\tve.Line = line\n205\t\t\t\t}\n206\t\t\t}\n207\t\t}\n208\t\n209\t\t// Extract column if available\n210\t\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"column \") {\n211\t\t\tparts := strings.Split(errMsg, \"column \")\n212\t\t\tif len(parts) > 1 {\n213\t\t\t\tcolStr := strings.Fields(parts[1])[0]\n214\t\t\t\tvar col int\n215\t\t\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(colStr, \"%d\", &col); err == nil {\n216\t\t\t\t\tve.Column = col\n217\t\t\t\t}\n218\t\t\t}\n219\t\t}\n220\t\n221\t\t// Extract contextual information\n222\t\tlines := strings.Split(content, \"\\n\")\n223\t\tif ve.Line > 0 && ve.Line <= len(lines) {\n224\t\t\t// Show the problematic line\n225\t\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"Line content: %q\", lines[ve.Line-1])\n226\t\n227\t\t\t// If we have a column, show a pointer to the error\n228\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 && ve.Column <= len(lines[ve.Line-1]) {\n229\t\t\t\tpointer := strings.Repeat(\" \", ve.Column-1) + \"^\"\n230\t\t\t\tve.Context += fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n             %s\", pointer)\n231\t\t\t}\n232\t\t}\n233\t\n234\t\t// Categorize the error based on message content\n235\t\tve.Type = categorizeError(errMsg)\n236\t\n237\t\treturn ve\n238\t}\n239\t\n240\t// Package yamlutil provides YAML validation utilities with detailed error reporting.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"gopkg.in/yaml.v3\"\n)\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of validation error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeSyntax     ErrorType = \"syntax\"     // YAML syntax errors (indentation, colons, etc.)\n\tErrorTypeStructure  ErrorType = \"structure\"  // Structural errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting)\n\tErrorTypeIO         ErrorType = \"io\"         // I/O errors (file not found, permissions)\n\tErrorTypeEmpty      ErrorType = \"empty\"      // Empty file or content\n\tErrorTypeDeprecated ErrorType = \"deprecated\" // Deprecated YAML features\n\tErrorTypeUnknown    ErrorType = \"unknown\"    // Uncategorized occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn   int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage  string    // Human-readable error message\n\tContext  string    // Contextual information about the error\n\tFilePath string    // Path to the file being validated\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: Line %d: %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.Type, ve.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Type, ve.Message)\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.Type))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d, Column %d\\n\", ve.Line, ve.Column))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Context != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.Context))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// ValidationResult represents the result of validating a YAML file.\ntype ValidationResult struct {\n\tFilePath string            // Path to the validated file\n\tValid    bool              // Whether validation passed\n\tErrors   []ValidationError // List of validation errors\n\tWarnings []ValidationError // List of validation warnings\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if there are any validation errors.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasErrors() bool {\n\treturn len(vr.Errors) > 0\n}\n\n// HasWarnings returns true if there are any validation warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) HasWarnings() bool {\n\treturn len(vr.Warnings) > 0\n}\n\n// ErrorSummary returns a formatted summary of all errors.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) ErrorSummary() string {\n\tif !vr.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Validation failed for %s:\\n\", vr.FilePath))\n\tfor i, err := range vr.Errors {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. %s\\n\", i+1, err.String()))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// WarningSummary returns a formatted summary of all warnings.\nfunc (vr ValidationResult) WarningSummary() string {\n\tif !vr.HasWarnings() {\n\t\treturn \"No warnings\"\n\t}\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"Warnings for %s:\\n\", vr.FilePath))\n\tfor i, warn := range vr.Warnings {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. %s\\n\", i+1, warn.String()))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Validator provides YAML validation functionality with detailed error reporting.\ntype Validator struct {\n\tstrict bool // Whether to use strict validation\n}\n\n// NewValidator creates a new YAML validator with default settings.\nfunc {\n\treturn &Validator{\n\t\tstrict: true,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateString validates YAML content from a string.\nfunc (v *Validator) ValidateString(yamlContent string) ValidationResult {\n\treturn ValidateStringWithPath(yamlContent, filePath string) ValidationResult {\n\tresult := ValidationResult{\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tValid:    true,\n\t\tErrors:   []ValidationError{},\n\t\tWarnings: []ValidationError{},\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for empty content\n\tif strings.TrimSpace(yamlContent) == \"\" {\n\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\tresult.Errors = append(result.Errors, ValidationError{\n\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeEmpty,\n\t\t\tMessage:  \"YAML content is empty\",\n\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\t})\n\t\treturn result\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse YAML to detect syntax errors\n\tvar node yaml.Node\n\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlContent), &node)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tresult.Valid = false\n\t\tve := v.parseYAMLError(err, filePath, converts a yaml.v3 error into a ValidationError with line/column info.\nfunc (v *Validator) parseYAMLError(err error, filePath, content string) ValidationError {\n\tve := ValidationError{\n\t\tType:     ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\tMessage:  err.Error(),\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse line and column from error message if available\n\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\n\t// Try to extract line number from common error patterns\n\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"line \") {\n\t\tparts := strings.Split(errMsg, \"line \")\n\t\tif len(parts) > 1 {\n\t\t\tlineStr := strings.Fields(parts[1])[0]\n\t\t\tvar line int\n\t\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(lineStr, \"%d\", &line); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tve.Line = line\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract column if available\n\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"column \") {\n\t\tparts := strings.Split(errMsg, \"column \")\n\t\tif len(parts) > 1 {\n\t\t\tcolStr := strings.Fields(parts[1])[0]\n\t\t\tvar col int\n\t\t\tif _, err := fmt.Sscanf(colStr, \"%d\", &col); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tve.Column = col\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract contextual information\n\tlines := strings.Split(content, \"\\n\")\n\tif ve.Line > 0 && ve.Line <= len(lines) {\n\t\t// Show the problematic line\n\t\tve.Context = fmt.Sprintf(\"Line content: %q\", lines[ve.Line-1])\n\n\t\t// If we have a column, show a pointer to the error\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 && ve.Column <= len(lines[ve.Line-1]) {\n\t\t\tpointer := strings.Repeat(\" \", ve.Column-1) + \"^\"\n\t\t\tve.Context += fmt.Sprintf(\"\\n             %s\", pointer)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Categorize the error based on message content\n\tve.Type = categorizeError(errMsg)\n\n\treturn ve\n}\n\n// categorizeError determines the error type based on the error message.\nfunc categorizeError(errMsg string) ErrorType {\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(errMsg)\n\n\t// Check for specific error patterns\n\tswitch {\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"could not find expected\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"did not find expected key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"found character that cannot start any key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"invalid indentation\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"duplicate key\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"mapping values are not allowed\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"unexpected end\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"unacceptable character\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"scanner error\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"unmarshal errors\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase strings.Contains(lower, \"cannot unmarshal\"):\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n\t}\n}\n\n// checkStructuralIssues checks for potential structural issues in the YAML.\nfunc (v *Validator) checkStructuralIssues(node *yaml.Node, content string) []ValidationError {\n\tvar warnings []ValidationError\n\n\t// Recursively check for structural issues\n\tv.checkNode(node, content, &warnings, make(map[string]bool))\n\n\treturn warnings\n}\n\n// checkNode recursively checks a YAML node for structural issues.\nfunc (v *Validator) checkNode(node *yaml.Node, content string, warnings *[]ValidationError, seenKeys map[string]bool) {\n\tif node == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Check for duplicate keys in mappings\n\tif node.Kind == yaml.MappingNode {\n\t\tkeys := make(map[string]bool)\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(node.Content); i += 2 {\n\t\t\tif i+1 < len(node.Content) {\n\t\t\t\tkeyNode := node.Content[i]\n\t\t\t\tif keyNode.Kind == yaml.ScalarNode && keyNode.Value != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tkey := keyNode.Value\n\t\t\t\t\tif keys[key] {\n\t\t\t\t\t\twarn := ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tType:     ErrorTypeStructure,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tMessage:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Duplicate key detected: %q\", key),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tLine:     keyNode.Line,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tColumn:   keyNode.Column,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tContext:  fmt.Sprintf(\"Key %q appears multiple times in mapping\", key),\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\t*warnings = append(*warnings, warn)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tkeys[key] = true\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Recursively check child nodes\n\tfor _, child := range node.Content {\n\t\tv.checkNode(child, content, warnings, seenKeys)\n\t}\n}\n\n// in the YAML data.\n11\ttype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n12\t\tFieldPath string // Dot-separated path to the missing field\n13\t}\n14\t\n15\t// Error implements the error interface.\n16\tfunc (e type.\n21\ttype TypeMismatchError struct {\n22\t\tFieldPath   string // Dot-separated path to the field\n23\t\tExpectedType string // Expected type description\n24\t\tActualType   string // Actual type description\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t// Error implements the error interface.\n28\tfunc (e *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n29\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch for field %s: expected %s, got %s\", e.FieldPath, e.ExpectedType, e.ActualType)\n30\t}\n31\t\n32\t// GetField retrieves a field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n33\t// Returns the value if found, or the provided defaultValue if the field is missing.\n34\t// For type safety, use the typed helpers (GetString, GetInt, GetBool) instead.\n35\t//\n36\t// The path parameter uses dot notation to traverse nested structures, e.g.:\n37\t//   - \"server.port\" accesses data[\"server\"][\"port\"]\n38\t//   - \"metadata.author\" accesses data[\"metadata\"][\"author\"]\n39\t//\n40\t// Returns defaultValue if any component of the path is missing or nil.\n41\tfunc GetField(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue interface{}) interface{} {\n42\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n43\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n44\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n45\t\t}\n46\t\treturn value\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t// GetString retrieves a string field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n50\t// Returns the string value if found and is a string, or defaultValue otherwise.\n51\t//\n52\t// Returns defaultValue if:\n53\t//   - The field is missing\n54\t//   - The field is nil\n55\t//   - The field is not a string type\n56\tfunc GetString(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue string) string {\n57\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n58\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n59\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n60\t\t}\n61\t\n62\t\t// Type assertion with conversion fallback\n63\t\tif str, ok := value.(string); ok {\n64\t\t\treturn str\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\t// Attempt to convert other types to string\n68\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n69\t\tcase int, int64, int32:\n70\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", v)\n71\t\tcase float64:\n72\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%g\", v)\n73\t\tcase bool:\n74\t\t\treturn strconv.FormatBool(v)\n75\t\tdefault:\n76\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n77\t\t}\n78\t}\n79\t\n80\t// GetInt retrieves an integer field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n81\t// Returns the integer value if found and is numeric, or defaultValue otherwise.\n82\t//\n83\t// Supports integer types (int, int64, int32) and float values that can be converted to int.\n84\t//\n85\t// Returns defaultValue if:\n86\t//   - The field is missing\n87\t//   - The field is nil\n88\t//   - The field cannot be converted to an integer\n89\tfunc GetInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue int) int {\n90\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n91\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n92\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n93\t\t}\n94\t\n95\t\t// Try direct int types first\n96\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n97\t\tcase int:\n98\t\t\treturn v\n99\t\tcase int64:\n100\t\t\treturn int(v)\n101\t\tcase int32:\n102\t\t\treturn int(v)\n103\t\tcase float64:\n104\t\t\t// YAML parses all numbers as float64, convert if it's a whole number\n105\t\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n106\t\t\t\treturn int(v)\n107\t\t\t}\n108\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n109\t\tcase float32:\n110\t\t\tif v == float32(int(v)) {\n111\t\t\t\treturn int(v)\n112\t\t\t}\n113\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n114\t\tcase string:\n115\t\t\t// Try to parse string as integer\n116\t\t\tif i, err := strconv.ParseInt(v, 10, 64); err == nil {\n117\t\t\t\treturn int(i)\n118\t\t\t}\n119\t\t\treturn the boolean value if found and is boolean, or defaultValue otherwise.\n127\t//\n128\t// Supports boolean types and string values that can be parsed as boolean\n129\t// (case-insensitive: \"true\", \"false\", \"yes\", \"no\", \"1\", \"0\").\n130\t//\n131\t// Returns defaultValue if:\n132\t//   - The field is missing\n133\t//   - The field is nil\n134\t//   - The field cannot be parsed as a boolean\n135\tfunc GetBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue bool) bool {\n136\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n137\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n138\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n139\t\t}\n140\t\n141\t\t// Direct boolean type\n142\t\tif b, ok := value.(bool); ok {\n143\t\t\treturn b\n144\t\t}\n145\t\n146\t\t// String to boolean conversion\n147\t\tif str, ok := 0.0\n166\t\tdefault:\n167\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n168\t\t}\n169\t}\n170\t\n171\t// HasField checks if a field exists at the given path in the YAML data.\n172\t// Returns true if the field exists and is not nil, false otherwise.\n173\t//\n174\t// The path parameter uses dot notation for nested access.\n175\tfunc HasField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) bool {\n176\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n177\t\treturn exists && value != nil\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t// GetRequiredField retrieves a field value, returning an error if the field is missing.\n181\t// Use this when a field is required for configuration validation.\n182\t//\n183\t// Returns the field value if found, or FieldNotFoundError if missing.\n184\tfunc GetRequiredField(data map[string]interface{}, path Package yamlutil provides debug-specific YAML utilities and field access helpers.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// FieldNotFoundError is returned when a requested field is not present in the YAML data.\ntype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n\tFieldPath string // Dot-separated path to the missing field\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (e *FieldNotFoundError) Error() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field not found: %s\", e.FieldPath)\n}\n\n// TypeMismatchError is returned when a field exists but is not of the expected type.\ntype TypeMismatchError struct {\n\tFieldPath   string // Dot-separated path to the field\n\tExpectedType string // Expected type description\n\tActualType   string // Actual type description\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (e *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch for field %s: expected %s, got %s\", e.FieldPath, e.ExpectedType, e.ActualType)\n}\n\n// GetField retrieves a field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n// Returns the value if found, or the provided defaultValue if the field is missing.\n// For type safety, use the typed helpers (GetString, GetInt, GetBool) instead.\n//\n// The path parameter uses dot notation to traverse nested structures, e.g.:\n//   - \"server.port\" accesses data[\"server\"][\"port\"]\n//   - \"metadata.author\" accesses data[\"metadata\"][\"author\"]\n//\n// Returns defaultValue if any component of the path is missing or nil.\nfunc GetField(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue interface{}) interface{} {\n\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n\tif !exists || value == nil {\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t}\n\treturn value\n}\n\n// GetString retrieves a string field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n// Returns the string value if found and is a string, or defaultValue otherwise.\n//\n// Returns defaultValue if:\n//   - The field is missing\n//   - The field is nil\n//   - The field is not a string type\nfunc GetString(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue string) string {\n\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n\tif !exists || value == nil {\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t}\n\n\t// Type assertion with conversion fallback\n\tif str, ok := value.(string); ok {\n\t\treturn str\n\t}\n\n\t// Attempt to convert other types to string\n\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n\tcase int, int64, int32:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", v)\n\tcase float64:\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%g\", v)\n\tcase bool:\n\t\treturn strconv.FormatBool(v)\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetInt retrieves an integer field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n// Returns the integer value if found and is numeric, or defaultValue otherwise.\n//\n// Supports integer types (int, int64, int32) and float values that can be converted to int.\n//\n// Returns defaultValue if:\n//   - The field is missing\n//   - The field is nil\n//   - The field cannot be converted to an integer\nfunc GetInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue int) int {\n\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n\tif !exists || value == nil {\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t}\n\n\t// Try direct int types first\n\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n\tcase int:\n\t\treturn v\n\tcase int64:\n\t\treturn int(v)\n\tcase int32:\n\t\treturn int(v)\n\tcase float64:\n\t\t// YAML parses all numbers as float64, convert if it's a whole number\n\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n\t\t\treturn int(v)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\tcase float32:\n\t\tif v == float32(int(v)) {\n\t\t\treturn int(v)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\tcase string:\n\t\t// Try to parse string as integer\n\t\tif i, err := strconv.ParseInt(v, 10, 64); err == nil {\n\t\t\treturn int(i)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetBool retrieves a boolean field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n// Returns the boolean value if found and is boolean, or defaultValue otherwise.\n//\n// Supports boolean types and string values that can be parsed as boolean\n// (case-insensitive: \"true\", \"false\", \"yes\", \"no\", \"1\", \"0\").\n//\n// Returns defaultValue if:\n//   - The field is missing\n//   - !exists || value == nil {\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t}\n\n\t// Direct boolean type\n\tif b, ok := value.(bool); ok {\n\t\treturn b\n\t}\n\n\t// String to boolean conversion\n\tif str, ok := \"disabled\":\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Numeric to boolean (0 = false, non-zero = true)\n\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n\tcase int, int64, path in the YAML data.\n// Returns true if the field exists and is not nil, false otherwise.\n//\n// The path parameter uses dot notation for nested access.\nfunc HasField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) bool {\n\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n\treturn exists && value != nil\n}\n\n// GetRequiredField retrieves a field value, returning an error if the field is missing.\n// Use this when a field is required for configuration validation.\n//\n// Returns the field value if found, or FieldNotFoundError if missing.\nfunc GetRequiredField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, error) {\n\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n\tif !exists || value == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, &FieldNotFoundError{FieldPath: path}\n\t}\n\treturn value, nil\n}\n\n// GetRequiredString(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (string, error) {\n\tvalue, err := GetRequiredField(data, path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn \"\", err\n\t}\n\n\tif str, ok := GetRequiredInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (int, error) {\n\tvalue, err := GetRequiredField(data, path)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to convert to int\n\tswitch {\n\t\t\treturn int(v), nil\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn 0, &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n\t\t\tActualType:   \"float\",\n\t\t}\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn 0, &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n\t\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetRequiredBool retrieves a boolean field value, &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n\t\tExpectedType: \"boolean\",\n\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateRequiredFields checks that all required field paths exist in the YAML data.\n// Returns a list of missing field paths. Empty list means all required fields are present.\n//\n// Each path in requiredFields uses dot notation for nested access.\n// Fields with nil values are considered missing.\nfunc ValidateRequiredFields(data map[string]interface{}, requiredFields []string) []string {\n\tvar missing []string\n\n\tfor _, path := range requiredFields {\n\t\tif !HasField(data, path) {\n\t\t\tmissing = append(missing, path)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn missing\n}\n\n// FieldRequirement represents a required field with its expected type.\ntype FieldRequirement struct {\n\tPath         string // Dot-separated path to the field\n\tExpectedType string // Expected type: \"string\", \"int\", \"bool\", or \"any\"\n\tOptional     bool   // If true, field is optional but must match type if present\n}\n\n// ValidateFieldRequirements validates field requirements with type checking.\n// Returns a list of validation errors. Empty list means all requirements passed.\n//\n// For each requirement:\n// - If Optional is false and field is missing → FieldNotFoundError\n// - If field exists but type doesn't match → TypeMismatchError\nfunc ValidateFieldRequirements(data map[string]interface{}, requirements []FieldRequirement) []error {\n\tvar errors []error\n\n\tfor _, req := range requirements {\n\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, req.Path)\n\n\t\t// Check if field is missing\n\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n\t\t\tif !req.Optional {\n\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, &FieldNotFoundError{FieldPath: req.Path})\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Skip type check if \"any\" type is specified\n\t\tif req.ExpectedType == \"any\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Type validation\n\t\ttypeMatch := false\n\t\tswitch req.ExpectedType {\n\t\tcase \"string\":\n\t\t\ttypeMatch = isString(value)\n\t\tcase \"int\":\n\t\t\ttypeMatch = isInt(value)\n\t\tcase \"bool\":\n\t\t\ttypeMatch = isBool(value)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Unknown type specification - skip validation\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !typeMatch {\n\t\t\terrors = append(errors, &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\t\t\tFieldPath:   req.Path,\n\t\t\t\tExpectedType: req.ExpectedType,\n\t\t\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn errors\n}\n\n// getFieldAtPath navigates through nested map structures using a dot-separated path.\n// Returns the value at the path and a boolean indicating if it exists.\nfunc getFieldAtPath(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, bool) {\n\tif data == nil || path == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, false\n\t}\n\n\t// Split path into components\n\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n\n\t// Start with the root map\n\tcurrent := interface{}(data)\n\n\t// Navigate through each level\n\tfor i, part := range parts {\n\t\t// Current level should be a map\n\t\tcurrentMap, ok := current.(map[string]interface{})\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Path component doesn't exist or parent isn't a map\n\t\t\treturn nil, false\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get the next level\n\t\tcurrent, ok = currentMap[part]\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\t// Field doesn't exist at this level\n\t\t\treturn nil, false\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If this is the last component, return the value\n\t\tif i == len(parts)-1 {\n\t\t\treturn current, true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Prepare for next iteration - current might not be a map, which will be caught next iteration\n\t}\n\n\t// Should not reach here if path is non-empty\n\treturn nil, false\n}\n\n// isString checks if a value is a string type.\nfunc isString(value interface{}) bool {\n\t_, ok := value.(string)\n\treturn ok\n}\n\n// isInt checks if a value is an integer or float64 representing an integer.\nfunc isInt(value interface{}) bool {\n\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n\tcase int, int64, int32:\n\t\treturn true\n\tcase float64:\n\t\treturn v == float64(int(v))\n\tcase float32:\n\t\treturn v == float32(int(v))\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// isBool checks if a value is a boolean type.\nfunc isBool(value interface{}) bool {\n\t_, ok := value.(bool)\n\treturn ok\n}\n\n// GetFieldWithType retrieves a field value and provides type information.\n// Returns the value, a boolean indicating if it exists, and a string describing its type.\nfunc GetFieldWithType(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, bool, string) {\n\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, false, \"missing\"\n\t}\n\tif value == nil {\n\t\treturn nil, true, \"nil\"\n\t}\n\treturn value, true, fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value)\n}\n","numLines":427,"startLine":1,"totalLines":427}}}

in the YAML data.\n11\ttype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n12\t\tFieldPath string // Dot-separated path to the missing field\n13\t}\n14\t\n15\t// Error implements the error interface.\n16\tfunc (e type.\n21\ttype TypeMismatchError struct {\n22\t\tFieldPath   string // Dot-separated path to the field\n23\t\tExpectedType string // Expected type description\n24\t\tActualType   string // Actual type description\n25\t}\n26\t\n27\t// Error implements the error interface.\n28\tfunc (e *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n29\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch for field %s: expected %s, got %s\", e.FieldPath, e.ExpectedType, e.ActualType)\n30\t}\n31\t\n32\t// GetField retrieves a field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n33\t// Returns the value if found, or the provided defaultValue if the field is missing.\n34\t// For type safety, use the typed helpers (GetString, GetInt, GetBool) instead.\n35\t//\n36\t// The path parameter uses dot notation to traverse nested structures, e.g.:\n37\t//   - \"server.port\" accesses data[\"server\"][\"port\"]\n38\t//   - \"metadata.author\" accesses data[\"metadata\"][\"author\"]\n39\t//\n40\t// Returns defaultValue if any component of the path is missing or nil.\n41\tfunc GetField(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue interface{}) interface{} {\n42\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n43\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n44\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n45\t\t}\n46\t\treturn value\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t// GetString retrieves a string field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n50\t// Returns the string value if found and is a string, or defaultValue otherwise.\n51\t//\n52\t// Returns defaultValue if:\n53\t//   - The field is missing\n54\t//   - The field is nil\n55\t//   - The field is not a string type\n56\tfunc GetString(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue string) string {\n57\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n58\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n59\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n60\t\t}\n61\t\n62\t\t// Type assertion with conversion fallback\n63\t\tif str, ok := value.(string); ok {\n64\t\t\treturn str\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\t// Attempt to convert other types to string\n68\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n69\t\tcase int, int64, int32:\n70\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", v)\n71\t\tcase float64:\n72\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%g\", v)\n73\t\tcase bool:\n74\t\t\treturn strconv.FormatBool(v)\n75\t\tdefault:\n76\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n77\t\t}\n78\t}\n79\t\n80\t// GetInt retrieves an integer field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n81\t// Returns the integer value if found and is numeric, or defaultValue otherwise.\n82\t//\n83\t// Supports integer types (int, int64, int32) and float values that can be converted to int.\n84\t//\n85\t// Returns defaultValue if:\n86\t//   - The field is missing\n87\t//   - The field is nil\n88\t//   - The field cannot be converted to an integer\n89\tfunc GetInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue int) int {\n90\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n91\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n92\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n93\t\t}\n94\t\n95\t\t// Try direct int types first\n96\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n97\t\tcase int:\n98\t\t\treturn v\n99\t\tcase int64:\n100\t\t\treturn int(v)\n101\t\tcase int32:\n102\t\t\treturn int(v)\n103\t\tcase float64:\n104\t\t\t// YAML parses all numbers as float64, convert if it's a whole number\n105\t\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n106\t\t\t\treturn int(v)\n107\t\t\t}\n108\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n109\t\tcase float32:\n110\t\t\tif v == float32(int(v)) {\n111\t\t\t\treturn int(v)\n112\t\t\t}\n113\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n114\t\tcase string:\n115\t\t\t// Try to parse string as integer\n116\t\t\tif i, err := strconv.ParseInt(v, 10, 64); err == nil {\n117\t\t\t\treturn int(i)\n118\t\t\t}\n119\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n120\t\tdefault:\n121\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n122\t\t}\n123\t}\n124\t\n125\t// GetBool retrieves a boolean field value from nested YAML data using a dot-separated path.\n126\t// Returns the boolean value if found and is boolean, or defaultValue otherwise.\n127\t//\n128\t// Supports boolean types and string values that can be parsed as boolean\n129\t// (case-insensitive: \"true\", \"false\", \"yes\", \"no\", \"1\", \"0\").\n130\t//\n131\t// Returns defaultValue if:\n132\t//   - The field is missing\n133\t//   - The field is nil\n134\t//   - The field cannot be parsed as a boolean\n135\tfunc GetBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string, defaultValue bool) bool {\n136\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n137\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n138\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n139\t\t}\n140\t\n141\t\t// Direct boolean type\n142\t\tif b, ok := value.(bool); ok {\n143\t\t\treturn b\n144\t\t}\n145\t\n146\t\t// String to boolean conversion\n147\t\tif str, ok := value.(string); ok {\n148\t\t\tswitch strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(str)) {\n149\t\t\tcase \"true\", \"yes\", \"1\", \"on\", \"enabled\":\n150\t\t\t\treturn true\n151\t\t\tcase \"false\", \"no\", \"0\", \"off\", \"disabled\":\n152\t\t\t\treturn false\n153\t\t\tdefault:\n154\t\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n155\t\t\t}\n156\t\t}\n157\t\n158\t\t// Numeric to boolean (0 = false, non-zero = true)\n159\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n160\t\tcase int, int64, int32:\n161\t\t\treturn v != 0\n162\t\tcase float64:\n163\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n164\t\tcase float32:\n165\t\t\treturn v != 0.0\n166\t\tdefault:\n167\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n168\t\t}\n169\t}\n170\t\n171\t// HasField checks if a field exists at the given path in the YAML data.\n172\t// Returns true if the field exists and is not nil, false otherwise.\n173\t//\n174\t// The path parameter uses dot notation for nested access.\n175\tfunc HasField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) bool {\n176\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n177\t\treturn exists && value != nil\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t// GetRequiredField retrieves a field value, returning an error if the field is missing.\n181\t// Use this when a field is required for configuration validation.\n182\t//\n183\t// Returns the field value if found, or FieldNotFoundError if missing.\n184\tfunc GetRequiredField(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, error) {\n185\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n186\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n187\t\t\treturn nil, &FieldNotFoundError{FieldPath: path}\n188\t\t}\n189\t\treturn value, nil\n190\t}\n191\t\n192\t// GetRequiredString retrieves a string field value, returning an error if missing or not a string.\n193\t// Returns the string value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not a string.\n194\tfunc GetRequiredString(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (string, error) {\n195\t\tvalue, err := GetRequiredField(data, path)\n196\t\tif err != nil {\n197\t\t\treturn \"\", err\n198\t\t}\n199\t\n200\t\tif str, ok := value.(string); ok {\n201\t\t\treturn str, nil\n202\t\t}\n203\t\n204\t\treturn \"\", &TypeMismatchError{\n205\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n206\t\t\tExpectedType: \"string\",\n207\t\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n208\t\t}\n209\t}\n210\t\n211\t// GetRequiredInt retrieves an integer field value, returning an error if missing or not numeric.\n212\t// Returns the integer value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not numeric.\n213\tfunc GetRequiredInt(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (int, error) {\n214\t\tvalue, err := GetRequiredField(data, path)\n215\t\tif err != nil {\n216\t\t\treturn 0, err\n217\t\t}\n218\t\n219\t\t// Try to convert to int\n220\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n221\t\tcase int:\n222\t\t\treturn v, nil\n223\t\tcase int64:\n224\t\t\treturn int(v), nil\n225\t\tcase int32:\n226\t\t\treturn int(v), nil\n227\t\tcase float64:\n228\t\t\tif v == float64(int(v)) {\n229\t\t\t\treturn int(v), nil\n230\t\t\t}\n231\t\t\treturn 0, &TypeMismatchError{\n232\t\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n233\t\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n234\t\t\t\tActualType:   \"float\",\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\tcase float32:\n237\t\t\tif v == float32(int(v)) {\n238\t\t\t\treturn int(v), nil\n239\t\t\t}\n240\t\t\treturn 0, &TypeMismatchError{\n241\t\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n242\t\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n243\t\t\t\tActualType:   \"float\",\n244\t\t\t}\n245\t\tdefault:\n246\t\t\treturn 0, &TypeMismatchError{\n247\t\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n248\t\t\t\tExpectedType: \"integer\",\n249\t\t\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n250\t\t\t}\n251\t\t}\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t// GetRequiredBool retrieves a boolean field value, returning an error if missing or not boolean.\n255\t// Returns the boolean value or FieldNotFoundError if missing, TypeMismatchError if not boolean.\n256\tfunc GetRequiredBool(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (bool, error) {\n257\t\tvalue, err := GetRequiredField(data, path)\n258\t\tif err != nil {\n259\t\t\treturn false, err\n260\t\t}\n261\t\n262\t\tif b, ok := value.(bool); ok {\n263\t\t\treturn b, nil\n264\t\t}\n265\t\n266\t\treturn false, &TypeMismatchError{\n267\t\t\tFieldPath:   path,\n268\t\t\tExpectedType: \"boolean\",\n269\t\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n270\t\t}\n271\t}\n272\t\n273\t// ValidateRequiredFields checks that all required field paths exist in the YAML data.\n274\t// Returns a list of missing field paths. Empty list means all required fields are present.\n275\t//\n276\t// Each path in requiredFields uses dot notation for nested access.\n277\t// Fields with nil values are considered missing.\n278\tfunc ValidateRequiredFields(data map[string]interface{}, requiredFields []string) []string {\n279\t\tvar missing []string\n280\t\n281\t\tfor _, path := range requiredFields {\n282\t\t\tif !HasField(data, path) {\n283\t\t\t\tmissing = append(missing, path)\n284\t\t\t}\n285\t\t}\n286\t\n287\t\treturn missing\n288\t}\n289\t\n290\t// FieldRequirement represents a required field with its expected type.\n291\ttype FieldRequirement struct {\n292\t\tPath         string // Dot-separated path to the field\n293\t\tExpectedType string // Expected type: \"string\", \"int\", \"bool\", or \"any\"\n294\t\tOptional     bool   // If true, field is optional but must match type if present\n295\t}\n296\t\n297\t// ValidateFieldRequirements validates field requirements with type checking.\n298\t// Returns a list of validation errors. Empty list means all requirements passed.\n299\t//\n300\t// For each requirement:\n301\t// - If Optional is false and field is missing → FieldNotFoundError\n302\t// - If field exists but type doesn't match → TypeMismatchError\n303\tfunc ValidateFieldRequirements(data map[string]interface{}, requirements []FieldRequirement) []error {\n304\t\tvar errors []error\n305\t\n306\t\tfor _, req := range requirements {\n307\t\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, req.Path)\n308\t\n309\t\t\t// Check if field is missing\n310\t\t\tif !exists || value == nil {\n311\t\t\t\tif !req.Optional {\n312\t\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, &FieldNotFoundError{FieldPath: req.Path})\n313\t\t\t\t}\n314\t\t\t\tcontinue\n315\t\t\t}\n316\t\n317\t\t\t// Skip type check if \"any\" type is specified\n318\t\t\tif req.ExpectedType == \"any\" {\n319\t\t\t\tcontinue\n320\t\t\t}\n321\t\n322\t\t\t// Type validation\n323\t\t\ttypeMatch := false\n324\t\t\tswitch req.ExpectedType {\n325\t\t\tcase \"string\":\n326\t\t\t\ttypeMatch = isString(value)\n327\t\t\tcase \"int\":\n328\t\t\t\ttypeMatch = isInt(value)\n329\t\t\tcase \"bool\":\n330\t\t\t\ttypeMatch = isBool(value)\n331\t\t\tdefault:\n332\t\t\t\t// Unknown type specification - skip validation\n333\t\t\t\tcontinue\n334\t\t\t}\n335\t\n336\t\t\tif !typeMatch {\n337\t\t\t\terrors = append(errors, &TypeMismatchError{\n338\t\t\t\t\tFieldPath:   req.Path,\n339\t\t\t\t\tExpectedType: req.ExpectedType,\n340\t\t\t\t\tActualType:   fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value),\n341\t\t\t\t})\n342\t\t\t}\n343\t\t}\n344\t\n345\t\treturn errors\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// getFieldAtPath navigates through nested map structures using a dot-separated path.\n349\t// Returns the value at the path and a boolean indicating if it exists.\n350\tfunc getFieldAtPath(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, bool) {\n351\t\tif data == nil || path == \"\" {\n352\t\t\treturn nil, false\n353\t\t}\n354\t\n355\t\t// Split path into components\n356\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n357\t\n358\t\t// Start with the root map\n359\t\tcurrent := interface{}(data)\n360\t\n361\t\t// Navigate through each level\n362\t\tfor i, part := range parts {\n363\t\t\t// Current level should be a map\n364\t\t\tcurrentMap, ok := current.(map[string]interface{})\n365\t\t\tif !ok {\n366\t\t\t\t// Path component doesn't exist or parent isn't a map\n367\t\t\t\treturn nil, false\n368\t\t\t}\n369\t\n370\t\t\t// Get the next level\n371\t\t\tcurrent, ok = currentMap[part]\n372\t\t\tif !ok {\n373\t\t\t\t// Field doesn't exist at this level\n374\t\t\t\treturn nil, false\n375\t\t\t}\n376\t\n377\t\t\t// If this is the last component, return the value\n378\t\t\tif i == len(parts)-1 {\n379\t\t\t\treturn current, true\n380\t\t\t}\n381\t\n382\t\t\t// Prepare for next iteration - current might not be a map, which will be caught next iteration\n383\t\t}\n384\t\n385\t\t// Should not reach here if path is non-empty\n386\t\treturn nil, false\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// isString checks if a value is a string type.\n390\tfunc isString(value interface{}) bool {\n391\t\t_, ok := value.(string)\n392\t\treturn ok\n393\t}\n394\t\n395\t// isInt checks if a value is an integer or float64 representing an integer.\n396\tfunc isInt(value interface{}) bool {\n397\t\tswitch v := value.(type) {\n398\t\tcase int, int64, int32:\n399\t\t\treturn true\n400\t\tcase float64:\n401\t\t\treturn v == float64(int(v))\n402\t\tcase float32:\n403\t\t\treturn v == float32(int(v))\n404\t\tdefault:\n405\t\t\treturn false\n406\t\t}\n407\t}\n408\t\n409\t// isBool checks if a value is a boolean type.\n410\tfunc isBool(value interface{}) bool {\n411\t\t_, ok := value.(bool)\n412\t\treturn ok\n413\t}\n414\t\n415\t// GetFieldWithType retrieves a field value and provides type information.\n416\t// Returns the value, a boolean indicating if it exists, and a string describing its type.\n417\tfunc GetFieldWithType(data map[string]interface{}, path string) (interface{}, bool, string) {\n418\t\tvalue, exists := getFieldAtPath(data, path)\n419\t\tif !exists {\n420\t\t\treturn nil, false, \"missing\"\n421\t\t}\n422\t\tif value == nil {\n423\t\t\treturn nil, true, \"nil\"\n424\t\t}\n425\t\treturn value, true, fmt.Sprintf(\"%T\", value)\n426\t}\n427\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_9a97ebedaecf4c089a8fe829","session_id":"39a5db4c-0fb5-4ac3-8529-ac46ee2a2472","uuid":"c39d6194-233f-4017-80ef-79df6c62a5f1","timestamp":"2026-07-09T15:46:25.395Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find YAML utility module"}

It provides three main categories of functionality:\n//\n// # File I/O Utilities\n//\n// The file utilities provide robust file reading with enhanced error handling:\n//\n//\timport \"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/yamlutil\"\n//\n//\t// Read a YAML file\n//\tcontent, err := yamlutil.ReadFile(\"config.yaml\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    if yamlutil.IsFileNotFoundError(err) {\n//\t        // Handle missing file\n//\t    }\n//\t    return err\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Check if a file exists\n//\tif yamlutil.FileExists(\"config.yaml\") {\n//\t    // File exists and is accessible\n//\t}\n//\n// # Parsing Utilities\n//\n// Parse YAML files into Go data structures with comprehensive error handling:\n//\n//\timport \"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/yamlutil\"\n//\n//\t// Parse into a map (dynamic structure)\n//\tdata, err := yamlutil.ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    // Handle YAMLParseError or FileError\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Parse into a specific struct\n//\ttype Config struct {\n//\t    Name  string `yaml:\"name\"`\n//\t    Port  int    `yaml:\"port\"`\n//\t}\n//\tparser := yamlutil.NewParser()\n//\tvar config Config\n//\tresult := parser.ParseFile(\"config.yaml\", &config)\n//\tif !result.Success {\n//\t    // Handle result.Error\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Parse YAML string content\n//\terr = parser.ParseString(\"key: value\", &data)\n//\n//\t// Find YAML files in a directory\n//\tfiles, err := yamlutil.FindYAMLFiles(\"/path/to/dir\")\n//\tfiles, err = yamlutil.FindYAMLFilesRecursive(\"/path/to/dir\")\n//\n// # Validation Utilities\n//\n// Validate YAML files with detailed error reporting including line numbers and context:\n//\n//\timport \"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/yamlutil\"\n//\n//\tvalidator := yamlutil.NewValidator()\n//\n//\t// Validate a file\n//\tresult := validator.ValidateFile(\"config.yaml\")\n//\tif !result.Valid {\n//\t    // Access detailed errors\n//\t    for _, err := range result.Errors {\n//\t        fmt.Printf(\"Line %d: %s\\n\", err.Line, err.Message)\n//\t    }\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Validate string content\n//\tresult = validator.ValidateString(\"key: value\")\n//\n//\t// Validate multiple files\n//\tresults := validator.ValidateMultipleFiles([]string{\n//\t    \"config1.yaml\",\n//\t    \"config2.yaml\",\n//\t})\n//\n// # Field Access Utilities\n//\n// Access nested YAML fields using dot notation with type-safe helpers:\n//\n//\timport \"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/yamlutil\"\n//\n//\t// Assuming data is parsed from: {server: {host: \"localhost\", port: 8080}}\n//\tdata, _ := yamlutil.ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n//\n//\t// Get field with default value\n//\thost := yamlutil.GetString(data, \"server.host\", \"default.host\")\n//\tport := yamlutil.GetInt(data, \"server.port\", 8080)\n//\tenabled := yamlutil.GetBool(data, \"server.enabled\", false)\n//\n//\t// Check if field exists\n//\tif yamlutil.HasField(data, \"server.port\") {\n//\t    // Field exists\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Get required field (returns error if missing)\n//\tport, err := yamlutil.GetRequiredInt(data, \"server.port\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    // Handle missing or wrong type\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Validate required fields\n//\tmissing := yamlutil.ValidateRequiredFields(data, []string{\n//\t    \"server.host\",\n//\t    \"server.port\",\n//\t})\n//\tif len(missing) > 0 {\n//\t    fmt.Printf(\"Missing required fields: %v\\n\", missing)\n//\t}\n//\n//\t// Advanced validation with type checking\n//\trequirements := []yamlutil.FieldRequirement{\n//\t    {Path: \"server.host\", ExpectedType: \"string\", Optional: false},\n//\t    {Path: \"server.port\", ExpectedType: \"int\", Optional: false},\n//\t}\n//\terrors := yamlutil.ValidateFieldRequirements(data, requirements)\n//\n// # Error Types\n//\n// The package provides several error types for different failure scenarios:\n//\n//   - FileError: File I/O errors with context (read, not found, permission denied)\n//   - YAMLParseError: YAML syntax errors with line/column information\n//   - ValidationError: Validation errors with detailed context\n//   - FieldNotFoundError: Required field is missing\n//   - TypeMismatchError: Field exists but has wrong type\n//\n// Example error handling:\n//\n//\tdata, err := yamlutil.ParseYAML(\"config.yaml\")\n//\tif err != nil {\n//\t    var parseErr *yamlutil.YAMLParseError\n//\t    if errors.As(err, &parseErr) {\n//\t        fmt.Printf(\"YAML error at line %d: %s\\n\", parseErr.Line, parseErr.Message)\n//\t    }\n//\t    var fileErr *yamlutil.FileError\n//\t    if errors.As(err, &fileErr) {\n//\t        fmt.Printf(\"File error: %s\\n\", fileErr.Op)\n//\t    }\n//\t}\n//\n// # {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create test file: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar config TestConfig\n\tresult := parser.ParseFile(testFile, &config)\n\n\tif !result.Success {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected successful parse with strict parser, got error: %v\", result.Error)\n\t}\n\n\tif config.Name != \"test\" {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected name='test', got '%s'\", config.Name)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestParseYAML(t *testing.T) {\n\ttmpDir := t.TempDir()\n\n\tt.Run(\"valid YAML file\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttestYAML := `\nkey1: value1\nkey2: value2\nnested:\n  item1: nested1\n  item2: nested2\n`\n\t\ttestFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"test.yaml\")\n\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte(testYAML), 0644); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create test file: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdata, err := ParseYAML(testFile)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected successful parse, got error: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif data[\"key1\"] != \"value1\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected key1='value1', got '%v'\", data[\"key1\"])\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnested, ok := data[\"nested\"].(map[string]interface{})\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected nested to be map[string]interface{}, got %T\", data[\"nested\"])\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif nested[\"item1\"] != \"nested1\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected nested.item1='nested1', got '%v'\", nested[\"item1\"])\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"empty file returns empty map\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttestFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"empty.yaml\")\n\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte(\"\"), 0644); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create test file: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdata, err := ParseYAML(testFile)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected empty file to return no error, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif data == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-nil empty map\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(data) != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected empty map, got %d items\", len(data))\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"whitespace-only file returns empty map\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\ttestFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"whitespace.yaml\")\n\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(testFile, []byte(\"   \\n\\t  \\n  \"), 0644); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create test file: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdata, err := ParseYAML(testFile)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected whitespace file to return no error, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif data == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected non-nil empty map\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(data) != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected empty map, got %d items\", len(data))\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"file not found returns FileError\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, err := ParseYAML(\"/nonexistent/file.yaml\")\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected error for missing file, got nil\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if it's a FileError by checking the error message\n\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n\t\tif !containsString(errMsg, \"read file\") && !containsString(errMsg, \"not found\") {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected file read error, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"invalid YAML syntax returns YAMLParseError\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tinvalidYAML := `key1: value1\nkey2: [unclosed bracket\nkey3: value3\n`\n\t\tinvalidFile := filepath.Join(tmpDir, \"invalid.yaml\")\n\t\tif err := os.WriteFile(invalidFile, []byte(invalidYAML), syntax error message, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"invalid YAML structure returns YAMLParseError\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// YAML with duplicated keys (which is TestValidator_HumanReadableMessages(t *testing.T) {\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\tyamlContent := `\nkey1: value1\n  invalid: indentation\nkey2: value2\n`\n\n\tresult := validator.ValidateString(yamlContent)\n\n\tif result.Valid {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected validation to fail\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify the error message is human-readable\n\terrMsg := result.Errors[0].Message\n\tif errMsg == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected error message to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Message should not be too cryptic\n\tif strings.Contains(errMsg, \"yaml:\") && len(errMsg) < 10 {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error message is too cryptic: \", errMsg)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify the full formatted error is readable\n\tformatted := result.Errors[0].String()\n\tif !strings.Contains(formatted, \"Error:\") ||\n\t\t!strings.Contains(formatted, \"Type:\") ||\n\t\t!strings.Contains(formatted, \"Location:\") {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Formatted error should have clear sections\")\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestValidator_WarningSummary(t *testing.T) {\n\tvalidator := NewValidator()\n\tresult := validator.ValidateString(\"key: value\")\n\tsummary := warnings\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t// Create a result with warnings\n\t\tresult := ValidationResult{\n\t\t\tFilePath: \"/test/file.yaml\",\n\t\t\tValid:    true,\n\t\t\tWarnings: []ValidationError{\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tType:    ErrorTypeStructure,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage: \"Duplicate key detected\",\n\t\t\t\t\tLine:    5,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tType:    ErrorTypeDeprecated,\n\t\t\t\t\tMessage: \"Deprecated YAML feature\",\n\t\t\t\t\tLine:    10,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tsummary := result.WarningSummary()\n\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(summary, \"Warnings\") {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected summary to contain 'Warnings', got: %s\", summary)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(summary, \"Duplicate key detected\") {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected summary to contain first warning message, got: %s\", summary)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(summary, \"1.\") && !strings.Contains(summary, \"2.\") {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected numbered warnings, got: %s\", summary)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestCategorizeError(t *testing.T) {\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname         string\n\t\terrorMsg     string\n\t\texpectedType ErrorType\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"could not find expected\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"yaml: line 1: could not find expected ':'\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"did not find expected key\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"did not find expected key\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"cannot start any key\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"found character that cannot start any key\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"invalid indentation\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"invalid indentation\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"duplicate key\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"duplicate key\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeStructure,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"mapping values not allowed\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"mapping values are not allowed here\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"unexpected end\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"unexpected end of document\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"unacceptable character\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"unacceptable character\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"scanner error\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"scanner error\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeSyntax,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"unmarshal errors\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"unmarshal errors\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeStructure,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"cannot unmarshal\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"cannot unmarshal !!str into []string\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeStructure,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:         \"unknown error\",\n\t\t\terrorMsg:     \"some unknown error\",\n\t\t\texpectedType: ErrorTypeUnknown,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := categorizeError(tt.errorMsg)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expectedType {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"categorizeError(%q) = %v, expected %v\", tt.errorMsg, result, tt.expectedType)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\n// Test acceptance criteria\nfunc TestAcceptanceCriteria_SyntaxValidationFunctional(t *testing.T) {\n\t// AC1: Syntax validation   map[string]interface{}{\"key\": \"value\"},\n\t\t\"empty_map\": map[string]interface{}{},\n\t}\n\n\ttests := []struct {\n\t\tname     string\n\t\tpath     string\n\t\texpected bool\n\t}{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"existing field\",\n\t\t\tpath:     \"existing\",\n\t\t\texpected: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"nil field returns false\",\n\t\t\tpath:     \"nil_val\",\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"nested existing field\",\n\t\t\tpath:     \"nested.key\",\n\t\t\texpected: true,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"nested missing field\",\n\t\t\tpath:     \"nested.missing\",\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"empty nested map returns false for missing key\",\n\t\t\tpath:     \"empty_map.key\",\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"missing top-level field\",\n\t\t\tpath:     \"missing\",\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tname:     \"empty path\",\n\t\t\tpath:     \"\",\n\t\t\texpected: false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, tt := range tests {\n\t\tt.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\tresult := HasField(data, tt.path)\n\t\t\tif result != tt.expected {\n\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"HasField(%q) = %v, expected %v\", tt.path, result, tt.expected)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t})\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestGetRequiredField(t *testing.T) {\n\tdata := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"existing\": \"value\",\n\t\t\"nil_val\":  nil,\n\t}\n\n\tt.Run(\"existing field\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tresult, err := GetRequiredField(data, \"existing\")\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected no error, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result != \"value\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected 'value', got: %v\", result)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"missing field\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, err := GetRequiredField(data, \"missing\")\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected error for missing field, got nil\")\n\t\t} else if _, ok := err.(*FieldNotFoundError); !ok {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected FieldNotFoundError, got: %T\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"nil field\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, err := GetRequiredField(data, \"nil_val\")\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected error for nil field, got nil\")\n\t\t} else if _, ok := err.(*FieldNotFoundError); !ok {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected FieldNotFoundError, got: %T\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestGetRequiredString(t *testing.T) {\n\tdata := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"string\": \"value\",\n\t\t\"number\": 42,\n\t}\n\n\tt.Run(\"existing string\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tresult, err := GetRequiredString(data, \"string\")\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected no error, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result != \"value\" {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected error for missing field\")\n\t\t} else if _, ok := err.(*FieldNotFoundError); !ok {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected FieldNotFoundError, got: %T\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"type mismatch\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, err := GetRequiredString(data, \"number\")\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected error for type mismatch\")\n\t\t} != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected no error, got: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result != 42 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected 42, got: %d\", result)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"int64 conversion\", func(t TypeMismatchError, got: %T\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"float32 that is not integer\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, err := GetRequiredInt(data, \"float32\")\n\t\tif err == nil field\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"type mismatch\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t_, err := GetRequiredInt(data, \"string\")\n\t\tif err == nil {\n\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected error for type mismatch\")\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestGetRequiredBool(t *testing.T) {\n\tdata := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"bool\":   true,\n\t\t\"string\": \"true\",\n\t}\n\n\tt.Run(\"existing bool\", func(t {\n\tdata := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"field1\": \"value1\",\n\t\t\"nested\": map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"field2\": \"value2\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"nil_field\": nil,\n\t}\n\n\tt.Run(\"all required fields present\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\trequired := []string{\"field1\", \"nested.field2\"}\n\t\tmissing := ValidateRequiredFields(data, required)\n\t\tif len(missing) != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected no missing fields, got: %v\", missing)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"some required fields missing\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\trequired := []string{\"field1\", \"missing_field\", \"nested.field2\"}\n\t\tmissing := ValidateRequiredFields(data, required)\n\t\tif len(missing) != 1 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected 1 missing field, got: %d\", len(missing))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif missing[0] != \"missing_field\" {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected 'missing_field', got: %q\", missing[0])\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"nil field is considered missing\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\trequired := []string{\"nil_field\"}\n\t\tmissing := ValidateRequiredFields(data, required)\n\t\tif len(missing) != 1 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected nil_field to be missing, got: %v\", missing)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"empty required list\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\trequired := []string{}\n\t\tmissing := ValidateRequiredFields(data, required)\n\t\tif len(missing) != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected no missing fields, got: %v\", missing)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\nfunc TestValidateFieldRequirements(t *testing.T) {\n\tdata := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"name\":  \"test\",\n\t\t\"count\": 42,\n\t\t\"flag\":  true,\n\t\t\"nested\": map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"value\": \"nested-value\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"wrong_type\": 123,\n\t}\n\n\tt.Run(\"all requirements met\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\trequirements := []FieldRequirement{\n\t\t\t{Path: \"name\", ExpectedType: \"string\", Optional: false},\n\t\t\t{Path: \"count\", ExpectedType: \"int\", Optional: false},\n\t\t\t{Path: \"flag\", ExpectedType: \"bool\", Optional: false},\n\t\t}\n\t\terrors := ValidateFieldRequirements(data, requirements)\n\t\tif len(errors) != 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected no errors, got: %v\", errors)\n\t\t}\n\t})\n\n\tt.Run(\"missing required field\", func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\trequirements := []FieldRequirement{\n\t\t\t{Path: \"missing\", ExpectedType: \"string\", Optional: false},\n\t\t}\n\t\terrors := ValidateFieldRequirements(data, requirements)\n\t\tif len(errors) != 1 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected 1 error, got: %d\", len(errors))\n\t\t} else if _, ok :=
